demos.report:
CT Report:
EXAMINATION:  CT STROKE BRAIN WO CONTRAST   CLINICAL HISTORY:  Neuro deficit  acute  stroke suspected    COMPARISON:  None.    TECHNIQUE: Noncontrast head CT performed using radiation dose reduction techniques.  Technical factors are evaluated and adjusted to ensure appropriate moderation of exposure.  Automated dose management technology is applied to adjust radiation exposure  while achieving a diagnostic quality image.    FINDINGS:    There is evidence of acute right MCA territorial infarction with loss of cortical gray-white matter differentiation of the mid lateral right precentral gyrus (series 303, image 45). No evidence of hemorrhagic transformation or mass effect.    There is small chronic infarction involving the right superior cerebellar hemisphere. There are Virchow-Robin perivascular spaces versus chronic lacunar infarctions in bilateral basal ganglia. There are mild nonspecific patchy areas of hypoattenuation in  periventricular, deep, and subcortical white matter, probably from chronic ischemic small vessel disease.    Ventricles are age-appropriate in size and configuration. No extra-axial fluid collection.    Visualized paranasal sinuses and mastoid air cells are clear. Bones, orbits, and soft tissues are unremarkable.    IMPRESSION:    1. Evidence of acute right MCA territorial infarction involving right precentral gyrus. No hemorrhagic transformation.    2. Small chronic right cerebellar infarction and mild chronic ischemic small vessel disease changes.    Findings were discussed with Dr. Bhavsar on 2/4/2022 5:49 PM, and she verbalized understanding of the report.    HMH-2UA70310L8

CTA Report:
EXAM: CT ANGIOGRAM HEAD W WO CONTRAST   CLINICAL HISTORY: Neuro deficit  acute  stroke suspected    TECHNIQUE:  Imaging of the intracranial circulation was obtained from the skull base to the vertex during the arterial phase of enhancement. Postprocessing was performed with MIP multiplanar and 3D reconstructed images.    CT scans are performed using radiation dose reduction techniques. Technical factors are evaluated and adjusted to ensure appropriate moderation of exposure. Automated dose management technology is applied to adjust radiation exposure while achieving a  diagnostic quality image.    COMPARISON:  CT stroke, performed concurrently      FINDINGS:    Scattered calcifications are identified within the bilateral cavernous to supraclinoid ICAs and left V4 with minimal luminal irregularity. Minimal flow is seen within the left V4 vertebral artery after the left PICA takeoff.    There is moderate luminal narrowing along a distal right posterior M2/M3 MCA branch, which may or may not account for the suspected recent right frontal convexity ischemia identified on CT brain performed concurrently.    Otherwise no hemodynamically significant stenosis is identified of the intracranial internal carotid arteries, middle cerebral arteries, anterior cerebral arteries, intracranial vertebral arteries, basilar artery or posterior cerebral arteries.    There is no aneurysmal dilatation or vascular malformation of the circle of Willis.    The major dural sinuses are opacified normally.      IMPRESSION:    1.Moderate luminal narrowing along a distal right posterior M2/M3 MCA branch, which may or may not account for the suspected recent right frontal convexity ischemia identified on CT brain performed concurrently.  2.Otherwise no acute, hemodynamically significant narrowing of the circle of Willis vessels.  3.Chronic findings as detailed above.    Findings were discussed with Dr. MIKAEL LEE LUCAS at 2/4/2022 6:09 PM who verbalized understanding.    1RM1RAD_PS17

CTP Report:
EXAMINATION: CT BRAIN PERFUSION W RECON   CLINICAL HISTORY: LVO    COMPARISON:  None    TECHNIQUE: Whole brain CT perfusion was performed after administration of IV contrast. Post processing was performed. Dose lowering technique(s) such as automated exposure control, iterative reconstruction, and mA and/or KV adjustment for patient size  was utilized for this examination. CT perfusion data was processed using automated software to assist stroke clinical decision-making.    FINDINGS:    Hypoperfusion volume (Tmax > 6 s, includes core and penumbra): 0 mL  Infarct core volume (CBF <30% of contralateral): 0 mL        IMPRESSION:    No perfusion deficits as described.        HMRM-WPHYRRS

demos.report:
CT Report:
EXAMINATION:  CT STROKE BRAIN WO CONTRAST   COMPARISON:  August 21, 2020.    CLINICAL HISTORY:  Neuro deficit  acute  stroke suspected    COMMENTS:  Axial noncontrast CT scan slices of the head were obtained. Sagittal and coronal reconstructions were obtained.    CT imaging was performed with iterative reconstruction technique and/or automated exposure control to reduce radiation dose.        FINDINGS:  There is subtle cortical and subcortical low density at the left parietal lobe which is a progressive finding compared to the previous study.    There is mild mucosal thickening in the visualized sinuses. There is calcific atherosclerotic change in the arteries at the skull base.    IMPRESSION:  No acute intracranial hemorrhage. Possible recent ischemic change at the left occipital lobe. Correlation with MRI is recommended.    The findings were discussed with Dr. Noffsinger who verbalized understanding with read back June 6, 2022 at 7:25 AM.      1RM1RAD_PS02

CTA Report:
EXAMINATION: CT ANGIOGRAM HEAD W WO CONTRAST   CLINICAL HISTORY: Neuro deficit  acute  stroke suspected    COMPARISON:  None    TECHNIQUE:  Imaging of the intracranial circulation was obtained from the skull base to the vertex during the arterial phase of enhancement. Postprocessing was performed with MIP multiplanar and 3D reconstructed images.  CT imaging was performed with  iterative reconstruction technique and/or automated exposure control to reduce radiation dose. CT angiography data was processed using artificial intelligence software to assist stroke clinical decision making with regards to the diagnosis of large  vessel occlusion.      FINDINGS:    The cervical, petrous, cavernous, clinoid and supraclinoid segments of internal carotid arteries are patent bilaterally. Small vascular calcifications along the supraclinoid segments are noted, however no aneurysms, dissection flaps or measurable  stenosis identified.    The major branches of the anterior and posterior circulations are patent without luminal irregularity. No basilar tip aneurysms. The V4 segments are symmetric and normal in appearance. No variant anatomy.    Limited examination of the brain demonstrates no evidence of acute intracranial abnormality.  Adequate opacification the dural venous sinuses revealing codominant transverse and sigmoid sinuses. No filling defects identified.      IMPRESSION:    Patent circle of Willis vasculature, without evidence of aneurysm or large vessel occlusion.          3NP1IMG_PS03

CTP Report:
EXAMINATION: CT BRAIN PERFUSION W RECON   CLINICAL HISTORY: ams  weakness    COMPARISON:  None    TECHNIQUE: Whole brain CT perfusion was performed after administration of IV contrast. Post processing was performed. Dose lowering technique(s) such as automated exposure control, iterative reconstruction, and mA and/or KV adjustment for patient size  was utilized for this examination.    FINDINGS:    No areas of abnormal hypoperfusion identified. Most recent CT head and CTA head demonstrate no evidence of cytotoxic edema or large vessel occlusion.    Hypoperfusion volume (Tmax > 6 s, includes core and penumbra): 0 mL  Infarct core volume (CBF <30% of contralateral): 0 mL  Mismatch (penumbra) volume: 0 mL  Mismatch ratio: 0    Other: None.        IMPRESSION:    No regions of hypoperfusion.          3NP1IMG_PS03

MRI Report:
EXAMINATION: MRI STROKE BRAIN WO CONTRAST   CLINICAL HISTORY: AMs    COMPARISON:  Head CT same day.    TECHNIQUE: Multiplanar and multisequence MRI imaging of the brain was obtained without contrast.      FINDINGS:    Bioccipital and posterior temporal lobe T2 FLAIR signal hyperintensity for example on images 15-17 of series 5 without significant mass effect. There is also trace restricted diffusion about the right occipital lobe. For example on series 3 image 16. No  associated mass effect or hemorrhagic component.    No evidence of acute intracranial hemorrhage, mass, mass effect, or midline shift.    Global cortical involutional changes are present accompanied by commensurate widening of an otherwise unremarkable ventricular system. There are bilateral periventricular and supraventricular white matter T2 FLAIR hyperintensities that are nonspecific  however likely related to microvascular ischemic changes. No abnormal susceptibility. Basal cisterns are clear. Major intracranial flow voids are maintained.    There are bilateral lens implants with the orbits and globes otherwise normal in appearance. Mucosal thickening of the right maxillary sinus. Mastoid air cells are clear.    IMPRESSION:    MR findings concerning for posterior reversible encephalopathy with superimposed right occipital lobe acute ischemic changes. No hemorrhagic component. No mass effect.      Findings were discussed with Nurse Meagan Carol at 6/6/2022 4:50 PM who verbalized understanding.        HMB-MJOEGDBZ

demos.report:
CT Report:
EXAMINATION:  CT STROKE BRAIN WO CONTRAST CLINICAL HISTORY:  Neuro deficit  acute  stroke suspected COMPARISON:  None. All CT images were acquired using low-dose technique with automated exposure control.  IMPRESSION:  Large occlusive hypodense clot in the M1 and M2 segment of the left MCA with acute infarction in the left basal ganglia and left corona radiata with is elevation of the cortical gray matter and an ASPECT score of approximately 8. There is no acute hemorrhage or mass effect. There is no midline shift. The ventricles are normal in size. The basal cisterns are patent. The calvarium is unremarkable. Findings discussed with  Shokraneh-Kenari, Keyon, MD on 12/18/2024 7:52 PM , and he verbalized understanding of the report. 1RM1RADPS82

CTA Report:
EXAMINATION:  CTA STROKE HEAD W WO CONTRAST CLINICAL HISTORY:  Neuro deficit  acute  stroke suspected COMPARISON:  None. TECHNIQUE: Head CTA with multi-planar MIP and volumetric rendering (3D) after bolus intravenous iodinated contrast administration was performed. All CT images were acquired using low-dose technique with automated exposure control. CT angiography data was processed using artificial intelligence software to assist stroke clinical decision making with regards to the diagnosis of large vessel occlusion.  IMPRESSION:  Occlusive thrombus in the M1 and M2 segments of the left MCA with the subtle cortical leptomeningeal collaterals. The ICAs ACAs and right MCA are patent. The posterior circulation is unremarkable. The posterior commuting arteries are not visualized. There is no definite aneurysm or vascular malformation. Findings discussed with  Shokraneh-Kenari, Keyon, MD  on 12/18/2024 7:56 PM , and the verbalized understanding of the report. 1RM1RADPS82

CTP Report:
EXAMINATION: CT BRAIN PERFUSION W RECON CLINICAL HISTORY: left m1 occlusion  COMPARISON:  None TECHNIQUE: Whole brain CT perfusion was performed after administration of IV contrast. Post processing was performed. Dose lowering technique(s) such as automated exposure control, iterative reconstruction, and mA and/or KV adjustment for patient size  was utilized for this examination. FINDINGS: The CTA of the head demonstrates large occlusive thrombus in the M1 and M2 segment of the left MCA with collateral cortical leptomeningeal circulation. The CT of the head demonstrates acute ischemic changes in the left basal ganglia and left corona  radiata with aspect score of about 8. Hypoperfusion volume in the left MCA territory (Tmax > 6 s, includes core and penumbra): 40 mL Infarct core volume (CBF <30% of contralateral): 0 mL Mismatch (penumbra) volume: 40 mL Mismatch ratio: Infinite Other: None.  IMPRESSION:  1. Perfusion deficit involving the left MCA with infarct core volume of 0 mL, mismatch volume of 40 mL, with a mismatch ratio of infinite. 1RM1RADPS82

MRI Report:
EXAMINATION:  MRI STROKE BRAIN WO CONTRAST CLINICAL HISTORY:  Neuro deficit  acute  stroke suspected, Stroke  follow up COMPARISON:  Head CT, CTA, and CTP on 12/18/2024, and head CT on 12/19/2024. TECHNIQUE: Noncontrast brain MRI with axial DWI, GRE, T2 FLAIR, and sagittal T1 FLAIR. FINDINGS: Early subacute left MCA territorial infarction involves primarily the left corona radiata and basal ganglia with small foci scattered in left insular and inferior frontal opercular juxtacortical region, associated with increased T2 FLAIR signal. No  significant mass effect. No evidence of hemorrhagic transformation. GRE shows linear branching susceptibility effect along the left M1 and M2 MCA segments, correlate for residual thrombus. Small foci of T2 FLAIR hyperintensity scattered in mostly bilateral frontal and to lesser degree parietal subcortical and deep white matter, probably sequela from mild chronic small vessel disease. Small cavum septi pellucidi. Ventricles, sulci, and cisterns are age-appropriate in size. No extra-axial fluid collection.  IMPRESSION:  Early subacute left MCA territorial infarction involves primarily left corona radiata and basal ganglia. No evidence of hemorrhagic transformation. Susceptibility effect along left M1 and M2 MCA segments, correlate for residual thrombus. HMB-MJOEGDBZ

demos.report:
CT Report:
EXAMINATION: CT STROKE BRAIN WO CONTRAST CLINICAL HISTORY: Neuro deficit  acute  stroke suspected COMPARISON:  None TECHNIQUE: Noncontrast CT of the brain was performed. Both soft tissue and bone reconstruction algorithms are interpreted. CT imaging was performed with iterative reconstruction techniques and/or automated exposure control to reduce radiation dose. FINDINGS: There is an acute infarct involving the entire left MCA and ACA territories (Aspects 0). There is mild local mass effect with some narrowing of the sulci. There is no midline shift. No hemorrhage is seen. The left carotid terminus and proximal left MCA M1 segment hyperdense suggesting thrombus. Moderate to extensive chronic small vessel ischemic changes are noted in the right cerebral white matter. Moderate right-sided involutional changes of brain are present. Chronic lacunar infarct is noted in the left cerebellar hemisphere. Visualized portions of the paranasal sinuses show no air-fluid level. Mastoid air cells are clear. No fracture or aggressive bony lesion is seen.   IMPRESSION:  Acute infarct involving the entire left MCA and ACA territories. The left carotid terminus and proximal left MCA M1 segment are hyperdense suggesting thrombus. Findings were discussed with Dr. EMILY LEEDE at 12/20/2024 6:34 PM who verbalized understanding. 1RM1RAD_PS46

CTA Report:
EXAMINATION: CTA STROKE HEAD W WO CONTRAST CLINICAL HISTORY: Neuro deficit  acute  stroke suspected COMPARISON:  Concurrent CT brain. TECHNIQUE:  Imaging of the intracranial circulation was obtained from the skull base to the vertex with contrast during the arterial phase of enhancement. Postprocessing was performed with MIP multiplanar and 3D reconstructed images.  CT imaging was  performed with iterative reconstruction technique and/or automated exposure control to reduce radiation dose. CT angiography data was processed using artificial intelligence software to assist stroke clinical decision making with regard to the diagnosis  of a large vessel occlusion. FINDINGS:  Internal carotid arteries and proximal branches: Right: Patent. Left: Acute thrombus extending from the petrous/intracranial segment of the ICA to the ICA terminus. Occluded MCA. Occluded A1 segment of the ACA. Reconstitution of the A2 segment by the right ACA and anterior communicating artery. Vertebral arteries: Patent. Basilar artery: Patent. Posterior cerebral arteries: Patent. A-Comm: Visualized. P-Comm: Right visualized. Left not well visualized. Limited evaluation of the venous sinuses demonstrates no evidence of thrombosis.  IMPRESSION:  1.    Extensive acute thrombus involving the left intracranial ICA with complete occlusion of the left MCA and A1 segment of the ACA. 2.    Patent right MCA and ACA without evidence of high-grade stenosis.  3.    Patent posterior circulation without evidence of high-grade stenosis. 1RM1RAD_PS77

CTP Report:
EXAMINATION:  CT BRAIN PERFUSION W RECON COMPARISON:  CT scan of the head obtained at 6:25 PM. CLINICAL HISTORY:  stroke COMMENTS:  Axial postcontrast CT scan slices of the head were obtained. Postprocessing for angiographic evaluation including 3-D reconstructions was obtained. FINDINGS:  Left-sided cord infarct by CBF less than 30% is 332 mL. Left-sided hypoperfusion by Tmax greater than 6 seconds is 343 mm. Mismatch volume is 11 mm. mismatch ratio is 1.  IMPRESSION:  Left-sided infarct and hypoperfusion as described above. 1RM1RAD_PS02

MRI Report:
EXAMINATION:  MRI STROKE BRAIN WO CONTRAST CLINICAL HISTORY:  stroke COMPARISON:  CT of the head dated December 20, 2024 at 6:36 PM TECHNIQUE: Multiplanar MRI imaging without IV Gadolinium was performed. FINDINGS: There is generalized brain parenchymal atrophy with white matter nonspecific T2 hyperintensities most likely to represent microvascular chronic ischemic disease. There is slight nonspecific ventriculomegaly most likely related to  central volume loss.  There is occlusion of the cervical and intracranial ICA with absence of flow signal in it with evidence of left ACA and left MCA mass effect territorial acute infarctions involving the majority of the left hemisphere with preservation of the left  thalamus and left occipital lobe/PCA territory. There is no associated mass effect. There is minimal hemorrhagic conversion involving the left parietal cortex.   There is no mass effect or midline shift. There is no hydrocephalus. The basal cisterns are patent. Visualized paranasal sinuses and mastoid air cells are clear. Bones, orbits, and soft tissues are unremarkable.  IMPRESSION:  Occlusion of the left cervical and intracranial ICA with massive acute territorial infarction involving the left MCA and left ACA territories. There is no associated mass effect. There is minimal hemorrhagic conversion involving the left parietal cortex.  There is no interval change from the recent head CT. 1RM1RADPS82

signature.instructions:
Label acute/recent stroke territory from radiology text.

/no_think

Return final answer only. Do not include hidden reasoning, scratchpad,
chain-of-thought, analysis, self-correction, or explanation.

Return only allowed labels. Use NONE only when the report has no
qualifying acute/recent/current territory evidence. Prefer the smaller
label set when evidence is ambiguous, and never include NONE with a real
territory label.

fields.description: A JSON-style list of labels chosen only from allowed_labels. Return labels only; no reasoning.
